lede: A look at January 17 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Peio Canales and Viktor Stålberg, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2010 Jos riots
events.desc: Rioting begins between Muslim and Christian groups in Jos, Nigeria, results in at least 200 deaths.

events.title: Mount Nyiragongo
events.desc: Mount Nyiragongo erupts in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, displacing an estimated 400,000 people.

events.title: Stockton schoolyard shooting
events.desc: Patrick Purdy opens fire at an elementary school in Stockton, California, killing five and wounding 31 others.

events.title: Dwight D. Eisenhower
events.desc: U.S. President Dwight D. Eisenhower delivers a televised farewell address to the nation three days before leaving office, in which he warns against the accumulation of power by the "military–industrial complex" as well as the dangers of massive spending, especially deficit spending.

events.title: Raoul Wallenberg
events.desc: Swedish diplomat Raoul Wallenberg is taken into Soviet custody while in Hungary; he is never publicly seen again.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Greek submarine Papanikolis captures the 200-ton sailing vessel Agios Stefanos and mans her with part of her crew.

events.title: Robert Falcon Scott
events.desc: British polar explorer Captain Robert Falcon Scott reaches the South Pole, one month after Roald Amundsen.

events.title: Lorrin A. Thurston
events.desc: Lorrin A. Thurston, along with the Citizens' Committee of Public Safety, led the Overthrow of the Kingdom of Hawaii and the government of Queen Liliʻuokalani.

events.title: James Cook
events.desc: Captain James Cook leads the first expedition to sail south of the Antarctic Circle.

events.title: Pope Gregory XI
events.desc: Pope Gregory XI reaches Rome, after deciding to move the Papacy back to Rome from Avignon.

deaths.text: Didier Guillaume, French politician, 25th Minister of State of Monaco
John J. McGinty III, American captain, Medal of Honor recipient

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Gamelbert of Michaelsbuch
Christian feast day: Charles Gore (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Jenaro Sánchez Delgadillo (one of Saints of the Cristero War)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of Pontmain
